title: Dependency Graphs, Incremental Builds, Caches, and Watch Mode · Failure Triage
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.
Key cached stages by canonical input bytes, relevant configuration, algorithm and tool versions, schema, and platform boundary.
Propagate changed, added, deleted, renamed, and dependency updates to every affected output and derived collection.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Dependency Graph
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 6812 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Record source, metadata, template, include, collection, taxonomy, route, asset, configuration, and tool-version dependencies per output.

Inspect the Dependency Graph trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clean Equivalence
steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-18khwd" that uses case 9116 to prove dependency-aware incremental output is equivalent to a clean build. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clean Equivalence.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Clean Equivalence into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Clean Equivalence, keep Clean Equivalence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-clean-equivalence-18khwd
# Competency: ssg-clean-equivalence.
# Case 9116: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_clean_equivalence_18khwd(outputs: int = 20, dependency_nodes: int = 37, invalidated: int = 6, stale_outputs: int = 0, clean_digest_equal: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_18khwd = "18khwd-dwhk81-9116"
    assert evidence_variant_18khwd
    evidence_axes_18khwd = (
        "reader18khwd",
        "source9116",
        "parsedwhk81",
        "route18khwd9116",
        "artifact911618khwd",
        "recoverydwhk819116",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_18khwd)) == 6
    if outputs < 1 or dependency_nodes < outputs:
        return False, dependency_nodes, "dependency-shape"
    if invalidated < 1 or invalidated > outputs:
        return False, invalidated, "invalidation-scope"
    if stale_outputs > 0:
        return False, stale_outputs, "stale-output"
    if not clean_digest_equal:
        return False, invalidated, "clean-drift"
    assert dependency_nodes >= outputs
    return True, invalidated, "clean-equivalent-incremental"
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Clean Equivalence structural check is only one gate. clean-equivalent incremental dependency engine must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
